channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Juice sold from New York to wineries in 12 states recalled over potentially deadly toxin
item.description: A wine juice wholesaler in New York has recalled a pumpkin wine juice sold to wineries in a dozen states because it contains a toxin that could be deadly.
item.dc:creator: Madalyn Buursma, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 1 person dead as violent storms, including tornadoes, wallop Midwest and South
item.description: Tornadoes and violent storms struck parts of the South and Midwest on Wednesday, killing at least one person, knocking down power lines and trees, ripping roofs off homes and shooting debris thousands of feet into the air.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN and HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Snatched pets and livestock deaths blamed on wolves raise alarms in rural New Mexico
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (AP) — Commissioners in a rural New Mexico county say pets are being snatched from front yards and livestock are being maimed and killed by endangered Mexican gray wolves that seem to have no fear of humans, prompting them to consider declaring a state of emergency.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 5 things to know about Republicans’ new blueprint to pass Trump's agenda
item.description: Senate Republicans on Wednesday rolled out a 70-page blueprint to advance President Trump’s tax agenda, along with directions that could pave the way for more than a trillion dollars in cuts to government spending.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Outrage grows over Maryland man's mistaken deportation to El Salvador prison
item.description: In the 22 days since Maryland resident Kilmar Abrego Garcia was mistakenly deported to a notoriously violent prison in El Salvador, his young son has sought comfort in the scent of his missing father's clothes.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Charges dismissed against Illinois man accused of assault by Rep. Nancy Mace
item.description: Federal prosecutors moved to dismiss charges against a man U.S. Rep. Nancy Mace (R-S.C.) accused of assaulting her during an event at the Capitol last year.
item.dc:creator: Sophie Brams, Fox 5

item.title: Senate budget blueprint empowers GOP chair to decide if Trump tax cuts add to deficit
item.description: Senate Republicans on Wednesday unveiled a 70-page budget resolution that they say would give Senate Budget Committee Chair Lindsey Graham (R-S.C.), a staunch ally of President Trump, the power to determine whether extending the 2017 Trump tax cuts officially adds to the federal deficit.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Gravedigger arrested after baby's corpse found on West Virginia cemetery's grounds
item.description: A gravedigger at a Cabell County cemetery was arrested on Wednesday after a baby's corpse from 1982 and a busted casket were found at the Baylous Cemetery last week.
item.dc:creator: Isaac Taylor, NewsNation

item.title: Police say the 2023 Nashville school shooter hid mental health issues from doctors and family
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — The shooter behind the 2023 Nashville elementary school attack that killed six people, including three children, had been obsessively planning it for years while hiding mental health issues from family and doctors, a police report released Wednesday reveals.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN MATTISE, KIMBERLEE KRUESI and TRAVIS LOLLER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump's 'Liberation Day' tariffs: Here's how much countries are getting hit
item.description: President Trump on Wednesday announced he was imposing reciprocal tariffs on dozens of nations, citing what he called years of unfair trade practices.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: District attorney lashes out at ICE for detaining suspect in Boston during a trial
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A district attorney in Boston harshly criticized the U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement on Wednesday after one of its agents detained a suspect while he was on trial. ICE agent Brian Sullivan took Wilson Martell-Lebron, 49, into custody last week as he was leaving court.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: United receives FAA certification on Starlink-equipped aircraft
item.description: United has received FAA approval for a Starlink-equipped plane. The first commercial flight is set for May.
item.dc:creator: Diana Falzone, NewsNation

item.title: Trump announces sweeping 'reciprocal' tariffs
item.description: President Donald Trump announced plans for sweeping tariffs Wednesday, saying “our country has been looted, pillaged, raped, plundered” by other nations.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Musk could be headed for a Washington exit after turbulent times at DOGE
item.description: President Trump has said that DOGE will end at some point and Musk will return to running his companies.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: HHS fires entire staff of program that helps low-income people afford heat and air conditioning
item.description: The Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) has fired all of the workers in its program that seeks to help low-income Americans pay their energy bills.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, WGN 9

item.title: Final report details investigation into deadly 2023 Covenant School shooting
item.description: The report touches on the shooter's planning as well as the details unveiled during the investigation.
item.dc:creator: Brittney Baird, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Supreme Court rules in favor of FDA in dispute over flavored vapes
item.description: The FDA has authorized the sale of only 34 e-cigarette products, and all except one have been tobacco flavored.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: Wild weather expected Wednesday gets a rare 'high-risk' designation
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — U.S. government forecasters are using a relatively rare “high-risk” designation — the highest category they use — to warn that a major tornado outbreak appears likely Wednesday in an area that's home to about 2.5 million people.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New documents describe physical condition of man allegedly held captive for decades in Connecticut
item.description: An investigator said the man's skin appeared "to be graying," and that his "physical condition resembles one who would be at a point at the end of their life.
item.dc:creator: Jenn Brink, KRON4

item.title: Nintendo Switch 2 launches in June with new 'Mario Kart World' game
item.description: Nintendo has announced a June 5 launch date for its latest gaming console, the Switch 2, which will introduce interactive chat and screenshare functions to connect gamers.
item.dc:creator: RYAN KRYSKA Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Democrats' win in Wisconsin court race a big loss for Elon Musk
item.description: Musk and his affiliated groups sunk at least $21 million into the normally low-profile race and paid three individual voters $1 million each for signing a petition in an effort to goose turnout in the pivotal battleground state contest.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Actor Val Kilmer dies at 65
item.description: Val Kilmer, a veteran actor known for his roles as Jim Morrison and Batman, has died on Tuesday, his family confirmed to the New York Times. He was 65. His daughter, Mercedes Kilmer, said her father died of pneumonia and was surrounded by family and friends.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Chow, KTLA 5

item.title: Man called 'Scamdaddy' sentenced for defrauding Utah-based credit unions out of $450K
item.description: A man who called himself "Scamdaddy" was sentenced for his involvement in a scheme to defraud Utah credit unions out of more than $450,000.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: U.S. soldier from Joliet dies during training exercise in Lithuania
item.description: One of the four U.S. soldiers who died in a training exercise in Lithuania last week was from Joliet.
item.dc:creator: Jenna Barnes, WGN 9

item.title: How to protect (and delete) your DNA data after 23andMe bankruptcy filing
item.description: The genetic testing company 23andMe recently filed for bankruptcy and announced it was looking to sell "substantially all of its assets." Here's what experts say that could mean for Utahns.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Booker sets record for longest Senate speech in history
item.description: Sen. Cory Booker (D-N.J.) on Tuesday set a new record for longest floor speech in Senate history, having held the floor for 25 hours and 5 minutes to decry potential GOP spending cuts in their looming tax bill and policies put in place by the Trump administration. Booker, the No.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, WGN 9

item.title: Dressing sold at Trader Joe's recalled over undeclared allergens
item.description: A California company has issued a voluntary recall for a dressing distributed to 18 states and sold under the Trader Joe's private label.
item.dc:creator: Mac Bell, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump administration admits it wrongly deported man to prison in El Salvador
item.description: The Trump administration acknowledged late Monday that it had mistakenly deported a Salvadoran man protected from removal, sending him to a facility in El Salvador where they argue they are unable to secure his return.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KTLA 5

item.title: Last US soldier found dead after Lithuania training accident. 3 others identified
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The final U.S. soldier who went missing in Lithuania has been found dead, bringing to an end a massive weeklong search for the four service members whose armored vehicle was pulled from a swampy training area, the U.S. military said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Federal prosecutors to seek death penalty for Luigi Mangione in UnitedHealthcare CEO's killing
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — U.S. Attorney General Pam Bondi said Tuesday that she has directed prosecutors to seek the death penalty against Luigi Mangione in the killing of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, following through on the president's campaign promise to vigorously pursue capital punishment.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: On the heels of a dry winter, firefighters around the US brace for wildfire risks
item.description: PRESCOTT, Ariz. (AP) — From the southwestern U.S. to Minnesota, Iowa and even parts of New Jersey, it seemed that winter never materialized.
item.dc:creator: TY ONEIL and SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Dressing sold at Trader Joe's in Florida recalled over undeclared allergens
item.description: A California company has issued a voluntary recall for a dressing distributed to 18 states and sold under the Trader Joe's private label.
item.dc:creator: Mac Bell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Dave Coulier shares big news about family, cancer fight
item.description: Dave Coulier, who shared he had been diagnosed with Non-Hodgkin lymphoma last year, had some exciting news to share on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Emily Smith, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Cornell student protester facing deportation leaves the US on his 'own terms' after losing faith
item.description: A Cornell University student facing deportation after his visa was revoked because of his campus activism said he decided to leave the United States. Momodou Taal, a citizen of the United Kingdom and Gambia, had asked a federal court to halt his detention.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL HILL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Missouri pastor gets deportation notice for sponsored son from Haiti
item.description: "Do we welcome immigrants in, if they come legally, and are pursing the citizenship process? Is that true or is that not?"
item.dc:creator: Hannah King, Fox 5

item.title: Booker talks through the night in marathon floor speech to protest Trump
item.description: Sen. Cory Booker (D-N.J.) pulled an all-nighter, delivering a marathon speech on the Senate floor that lasted well into Tuesday morning in a push to combat the Trump administration’s policies. Booker, a member of Democratic leadership, rose to speak around 7 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Girl, 9, dies after procedure at California dental office
item.description: A report from the San Diego County Medical Examiner's Office claims the girl was discharged from the facility and remained asleep on the ride home.
item.dc:creator: Delaney White, WGN 9
